rationale.R1.1: Section asserts two layered contribution claims — the SG-Constraint suite as 'central contribution' and the tube-MPC dominance finding as 'principal methodological contribution' — creating a framing tension that demands scrutiny of the gap statement and whether both claims are coherently scoped.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain (prior benchmarks inadequate → suite fixes comparison → tube-MPC result revealed → robust-control wins) must be checked for internal consistency; in particular the inference that ad-hoc benchmarks 'failed to reveal' the gap requires logical support not yet provided.
rationale.R1.3: The section is numerically dense: 0.81 ± 0.03 vs. 0.62 ± 0.08 vs. 0.47 ± 0.11, 'one to two orders of magnitude' lower violation rates on tasks 7–10, and the (1 − violation_rate)² rubric weighting. Every headline claim rests on these figures; failure to support them invalidates the section's conclusions.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty pivots on two fronts: the benchmark filling a gap in standardized comparison, and the learned-disturbance-set tube-MPC construction credited to Park and Wei (2024). The delta over prior benchmarks and prior controllers must be assessed to validate the 'dominance' framing.
rationale.R1.7: Park and Wei (2024) is the sole named prior work and is load-bearing for the novelty claim; 'standard MPC literature' for constraint formulations is uncited. Both require accuracy and coverage verification.
